Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The task is to analyze an event log for an insurance claims processing system to identify cases with performance issues and determine the root causes based on attributes like Resource Region and Complexity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To do this I need to calculate the total time each case takes from submission to closure The event log has each activity with a timestamp so for each case I should find the start time Submit Claim and the end time Close Claim then compute the duration

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Submit on 2024 04 01 09 00 Close on 2024 04 01 10 30 Thats 1 5 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Submit 09 05 Close 2024 04 02 11 00 Thats 1 day and 1 hour 55 minutes so roughly 27 9 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Submit 09 10 on day 1 Close 09 30 on day 3 Thats two days and 30 minutes so 50 5 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Submit 09 20 Close 10 45 same day About 1 hour 25 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Submit 09 25 on day 1 Close 14 30 on day 4 Thats three days and 5 hours 5 minutes So 77 08 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the cases with longer durations are 2002 2003 and especially 2005 Cases 2001 and 2004 are quick

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2002 Region B Medium Complexity The process involved a Request Additional Documents step once

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2003 Region A High Complexity Had two Request Additional Documents events

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2005 Region B High Complexity Had three Request Additional Documents events

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the longer cases are those with higher complexity Medium and High and possibly certain regions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 A High 50 5 hours Case 2005 B High 77 08 hours So Region Bs high complexity case took longer

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But sample size is small here

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe certain resources are slower

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa handled both 2002 and 2005 But in 2002 it was Medium complexity with one request and 2005 High with three So maybe the resource isnt the issue but the complexity and number of requests

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The number of times additional documents are requested correlates with longer times High complexity cases have more such requests

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2001 Low 0 requests Duration 1 5h Case 2002 Medium 1 request Duration 28h Case 2003 High 2 requests Duration 50 5h Case 2004 Low 0 requests 1 4h Case 2005 High 3 requests Duration 77h

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So theres a clear correlation between the number of Request Additional Documents and the duration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Each request adds time because the process has to wait for the customer to provide documents and then the adjuster has to review them again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Region B might have longer processing times but with the data given its hard to say

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The high duration cases are 2003 A and 2005 B So both regions have high complexity cases taking longer

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Maybe Region Bs process is slower or perhaps the adjuster in Region B Lisa took more time per request

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But without more data on how long each request took its hard to tell Alternatively the number of requests is the main driver

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Case 2003 after the first Request Additional Documents at 11 00 on day 1 the next event is another Request at 17 00 same day Then the next step was approval on day 2 at 16 00 which is a day later

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Case 2005 between the third Request on day 3 at 15 00 and Approval on day 4 at 10 00 thats 19 hours So each Request step introduces a waiting period

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the more Request steps the longer the case takes High complexity leads to more Requests hence longer duration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The primary root cause is the complexity level leading to multiple document requests each adding significant wait time Secondary factors could be regional inefficiencies but the data is insufficient to confirm that

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Streamline the document request process for high complexity claims Maybe pre identify required documents upfront to reduce multiple requests

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Implement a system to track and expedite high complexity claims ensuring quicker turnarounds for each step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Provide additional training or resources to adjusters handling high complexity cases to reduce processing time per request

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze regional performance to identify if certain regions need process improvements or resource allocation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Use automation or AI to pre emptively request common additional documents for high complexity claims reducing the need for multiple iterations
